summary: Mass tracks of unique m/z values are extracted from each mzML data file, then aligned into a MassGrid.
Retention time is calibrated for each sample to a common reference sample.
For each m/z value, corresponding mass tracks from all sample files are summarized into one composite mass track.
The sample with the highest number of anchor mass tracks is designated as the reference sample.
If the sample number is no more than 10, this is considered a small study and pairwise alignment is performed.
A set of landmark elution peaks are determined in the reference sample by the criterion: mSelectivity > 0.99.
The default min_peak_height for landmark peaks is 1e5. The default min_peak_height for Orbitrap data is 1e5.
Landmark peak prominence must be greater than 20% of peak height.
LOWESS regression is used to obtain a function describing the relationship of RT values between good_landmark_peaks and selected_reference_landmark_peaks.
A 10% extension out of both ends is added as boundaries for LOWESS regression convergence.
